ingredients.title: For the Fish
items: White fish fillets (cod, haddock, or halibut) – 800 g (approx. 200 g each, skinless & boneless)
Cold sparkling water (or chilled beer for beer-batter style) – 200 ml
Freshly ground black pepper – to taste
Oil (neutral, high smoke point: sunflower or canola) – for deep-frying

ingredients.title: For the Tartar Sauce
items: Sea salt & white pepper – to taste

ingredients.title: Garnishes & Accompaniments
items: Hand-cut fries or roasted baby potatoes (optional)

instruction.title: Prepare the Tartar Sauce
steps: In a bowl, combine mayonnaise, chopped cornichons, capers, shallot, parsley, dill, lemon juice, and Dijon mustard.
Season lightly with sea salt and white pepper.
Mix until smooth and well combined. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to let the flavors meld.

instruction.title: Prepare the Batter
steps: In a chilled mixing bowl, whisk together flour, cornstarch, baking powder, and a pinch of salt.
Make a well in the center and add the egg.
Slowly whisk in the cold sparkling water (or beer) until the batter is smooth and slightly thick but still flowing.
Tip: Keep the batter cold for extra crispiness.

instruction.title: Prepare the Fish
steps: Pat fish fillets completely dry with paper towels.
Season lightly with salt and pepper.
Dust each fillet in a little plain flour to help the batter stick.

instruction.title: Frying
steps: Heat oil in a deep pan or fryer to 180°C (350°F).
Dip each fillet into the batter, allowing excess to drip off.
Gently lower into hot oil and fry for 4–6 minutes, until golden brown and crisp.
Drain on a rack or paper towels.

instruction.title: Plating
steps: Place fish fillet on a warm plate.
Serve with a quenelle or ramekin of tartar sauce.
Garnish with lemon wedge and microgreens/parsley.
Optional: Add crispy fries or roasted baby potatoes for a full continental-style presentation.

chefTips: Use sparkling water/beer ice cold—it ensures a light, airy batter.
Do not overcrowd the fryer; fry in batches for even cooking.
Rest fish on a rack instead of paper towels to keep it crisp.
For extra refinement: add a touch of finely grated lemon zest into the tartar sauce.
